title: D-15: ⭐ HIGH PRIORITY — Topic Prepare (Accepted→Prepared) has no UI affordance. POST /api/topics/{id}/prepare (BL-036 / AC-035) shipped backend-only: the SPA never calls it (only accept/return are wired, in
activation_trigger: **Activate immediately.** Fix (frontend-only, no ADR): `usePrepareTopic` + a **"Mark prepared"** button on an `Accepted` topic detail (`TopicDetail.tsx` `.dt-actions`); **gating = show-and-enforce** (render for any Accepted topic, backend 403s — the owner is often a plain Member, so a role gate would hide it from the right person); **invalidate three query keys** (backlog + `['topics','prepared']` + `['topics','detail',key]`); a **"Prepared" badge** on kanban cards (Accepted & Prepared share the `accepted` bucket, `topicMeta.ts`, so Prepared is otherwise invisible); reword the agenda-pool empty-state to point to the action. **Optional W4-completeness:** notify the Secretary on prepare (`PrepareTopicHandler` only audits today; the `INotificationChannel` seam exists). **Open sub-questions (raise an OQ if pursued):** the domain has **no "un-prepare" / defer-from-Prepared** transition (a mis-prepared topic is stuck until scheduled); and **design-update-owed** (the design omits Prepare). **✅ RESOLVED 2026-07-09 (Tier 3):** "Mark prepared" button on the Accepted-topic detail (`usePrepareTopic`, show-and-enforce, inline 403) + Prepared kanban badge + reworded agenda empty-state + `PrepareTopicHandler` Secretary notification (skip-self); `core-loop.spec` now **clicks the button** instead of a direct-HTTP prepare (the swapped spec was run locally against the live stack — green; CI re-validates on the PR), and `TopicApiTests` proves the new DI seams. Un-prepare gap → **OQ-049** (default = defer). Design-update-owed still stands.
custom_attributes: {"v1": {"id": "D-15", "type": "Tech-debt", "phase1_handling": "E2E `core-loop.spec` performs prepare via **direct HTTP**, masking the gap (see AC-035 audit note). **No user-facing workaround exists.**", "target_phase": "Phase 2 (fix early — highest-priority defect)", "raw_status": "done", "note": "severity not tracked in v1 register; defaulted to medium"}}

title: D-16: Audit immutability + verify hardening: (1) a DB-permission grant that makes the audit schema INSERT/SELECT-only (block UPDATE/DELETE for out-of-app / DBA writes), and (2) a nightly Hangfire chain-veri
custom_attributes: {"v1": {"id": "D-16", "type": "Tech-debt", "phase1_handling": "App-layer immutability holds (`AuditEvent` has no mutation surface — no public setters, no Update/Delete path; UNIQUE `PreviousHash`/`Hash` non-forking indexes); tamper is **detectable** via the on-demand `AuditChainVerifier`. AC-017/018/019/020 are `Met` on this basis (ADR-0026/0027, PR #105).", "target_phase": "Phase 2 (P16 hardening)", "raw_status": "done", "note": "severity not tracked in v1 register; defaulted to medium"}}

custom_attributes: {"v1": {"id": "D-17", "type": "Tech-debt", "phase1_handling": "Audit **is** emitted for every child state change (compliance holds); only the before/after enrichment is absent. Surfaced 2026-07-13 from the P15a build.", "target_phase": "Phase 2 (P16 or a Research follow-up)", "raw_status": "open", "note": "severity not tracked in v1 register; defaulted to medium"}}

title: D-18: Audit-append concurrency contention. SqlAuditSink takes no application-level lock: two genuinely concurrent state-changing requests both read the same chain tip (TipHashAsync) and race the audit.Audit
custom_attributes: {"v1": {"id": "D-18", "type": "Tech-debt", "phase1_handling": "Audited write-commands now serialize globally — negligible at ≤20 users; partition the lock per-stream only if throughput ever bites.", "target_phase": "Fixed 2026-07-13 (ADR-0028; plan `~/.claude/plans/d18-audit-append-concurrency-plan.md`)", "raw_status": "done", "note": "severity not tracked in v1 register; defaulted to medium"}}
